Dev.releaseNotes: `misc`: **Release Management Fixes**.

`feat` (`abstractions`): **Add plugin-registered resource resolver support**.

`chore` (`abstractions`): **Bump abstractions version to v6.0.0-alpha.73**.
`misc`: **Cleanup**.
`misc`: **Work on Release Management**.

`feat` (`abstractions`): **Add nb/no language codes**.

`fix`: **Expose raw language code to plugins**.

`chore`: **Make media info converters/helpers nullable**.

`misc`: **Allow nullable path into ensure usable path**.

`feat`: **Expose method to get media info obj. from raw json or path**.

`chore` (`abstractions`): **Bump abstractions version to v6.0.0-alpha.72**.

`fix`: **Always resolve home path**.

`fix`: **Add default impl. for Details and PostInit for IQueueJob**.

`chore` (`queue`): **Bump queue processor version to v6.0.0-alpha.6**.
`fix`: **Fix an edge case in Release Management**.

`feat` (`anidb`): **Add relation verification for alternative setting/version types**:

AniDB's HTTP API has returned incorrect relation types (Alternative
Setting vs Alternative Version) for decades. This adds a two-tier
verification system to detect and correct these faulty relations.

- Added `verified` attribute parsing from `<relatedanime>` XML nodes
  for when the HTTP API endpoint supports it, defaulting faulty types to
  unverified if not.
- Added `Verified` column to `AniDB_Anime_Relation`, exposed via
  `IRelatedMetadata.Verified` and APIv3 DTOs.
- Added UDP `RequestGetAnime` command and `VerifyAniDBRelationsJob`
  to fetch correct relation data from the AniDB UDP API.
- Added pre-check and post-check logic to resolve and propagate
  verified relations bidirectionally, minimizing UDP round-trips.
- Added `VerifyAllRelations` admin action to batch-verify all
  unverified relations across the database.

Closes #1241

`fix`: **Mark nullable fields in APIv2 as nullable**.

`fix` (`db`): **Widen `TMDB_Show`/`TMDB_Movie` `Genres` column on MySQL and SQL Server**:

`Genres` was left at its original 128-character cap while `Keywords`,
`ContentRatings`, and `ProductionCountries` were all widened in later
migrations, leaving it exposed to the same `Data too long` failure
class as `Keywords` (fixed in `f0761fc75`).

`fix`: **Correct raw relation mapping based on probing the UDP API**.

`refactor` (`api`): **Generalize range compression and use it for AniDB group status EpisodeRange**.

`chore` (`abstractions`): **Bump abstractions version to v6.0.0-alpha.71**.

`chore`: **Normalize spacing after commas in log message templates**. (_Skip CI_)

`chore`: **Move nullable disabled declaration**.

`chore`: **Sort imports and remove unnecessary imports**.

`feat`: **Expose AniDB release group status via abstraction and v3 API**:

Adds `IAnidbAnime.ReleaseGroupStatuses` to the plugin abstraction, reusing
`IAnidbEpisode` and `IReleaseGroup` for resolved references while keeping raw
scalars available for plugins that don't need them, and surfaces the same
data read-only via `GET /api/v3/Series/{seriesID}/AniDB/ReleaseGroupStatus`
and `GET /api/v3/Series/AniDB/{anidbID}/ReleaseGroupStatus` (closes #1359).

Also removes the duplicate `Shoko.Server.Providers.AniDB.EpisodeType` enum
now that its values are identical to the abstraction's `EpisodeType`, in
line with already dropping the redundant APIv3 episode type.

`chore` (`abstractions`): **Bump abstractions version to v6.0.0-alpha.70**.

`fix` (`scheduling`): **Restore abstraction-typed DI for `VideoReleaseService` jobs**:

`72142566f` converted job classes to primary constructors and, for
`AnidbProcessFileJob`, `FinalizeReleaseSearchJob`, and
`ProcessReleaseProviderJob`, switched the constructor parameter from
`IVideoReleaseService` to the concrete `VideoReleaseService`. Only the
interface is registered in the DI container (`SystemService.cs`), so
those jobs failed to activate at runtime.

Reverted the constructor parameters to `IVideoReleaseService` and added
a `VideoReleaseService` field cast once at construction, used only for
the impl-only members (`FireSearchCompleted`, the `matchAttempt`
overload of `SaveReleaseForVideo`) that aren't exposed on the
interface.

`refactor`: **Remove `MimeDetective` and use `IsAllowedVideoExtension` for file type checks**:

Replaced the MimeDetective magic-byte content inspector with a call to
`VideoService.IsAllowedVideoExtension`, which checks against the
user-configurable `ImportSettings.VideoExtensions` list. A new video
format no longer requires a code change to be recognized by the hashing
pipeline.

`chore`: **Add missing video extensions to `ContentTypeHelper`**:

Added .asf, .divx, .f4v, .m1v, .m2ts, .m2v, .mk3d, .mts, .mxf, .nsv,
.nut, .ogm, .roq, .tp, .trp, .ts, .xvid
`chore` (`abstractions`): **Bump abstractions version to v6.0.0-alpha.69**.

`refactor` (`scheduling`): **Convert job classes to primary constructors**:

Converted all job classes to C# primary constructors, eliminating the
`protected` parameterless constructors that were preventing DI from
creating job instances on some jobs.

- Removed `protected` parameterless constructors from all job classes,
  which fixes DI activation failures.
- Replaced static service locator patterns (`ISystemService.StaticServices`,
  `RepoFactory`, `ISettingsProvider.Instance`) with proper DI in image
  and token cleanup jobs.
- Added `[JobKeyMember] Key` property to `DeleteRedundantReleasesJob`,
  fixing the same dedup issue `AVDumpFilesJob` had with dict/list
  parameters.
- Added `.WhereNotNull()` to episode iteration in `AddFileToMyListJob`
  for null safety.
- Removed unused dependencies from `ScanForMissingReleaseInfoJob` and
  `GetAniDBReleaseGroupJob`.
- Deleted the unused `IImageDownloadJob` interface.
- Fixed nullability annotations and compiler warnings for
  post-construction property initialization patterns.

`refactor` (`abstractions`): **Replace ad-hoc `AniDB_Anime.Resources` with shared `IWithResources` abstraction**:

Promoted the informal `(string Type, string Name, string URL)` tuple list
on `AniDB_Anime` into a first-class `Resource`/`ResourceType`/`IWithResources`
shape in `Shoko.Abstractions`, wired into `ISeries`, `ICreator`, and `IMovie`
so it's reusable across AniDB and TMDB providers instead of being AniDB-only.

- Added `ResourceType` (`Website`, `Streaming`, `Metadata`, `CrossReference`)
  and a sealed `Resource` record with a nullable ISO 639-1 `LanguageCode`,
  since a shared `Website` type can no longer distinguish an EN/JP official
  site by type alone.
- Converted `AniDB_Anime.Resources` to the new shape and folded in MyAnimeList
  IDs as `CrossReference` entries.
- `AnimeSeries.Resources` now aggregates across the linked AniDB anime and all
  linked TMDB shows/movies, mirroring the existing cast/crew/studio pattern.
- `AniDB_Creator` exposes its homepage/wiki URLs as resources (existing scalar
  fields kept for compatibility).
- `TMDB_Show`/`TMDB_Movie` expose their `TvdbShowID`/`ImdbMovieID` as
  cross-reference resources. `TMDB_Person` gained a new `ImdbPersonID` field,
  populated via TMDB's `PersonMethods.ExternalIds`, with matching DB patches
  for SQLite/MySQL/SQL Server.
- The v3 `Resource` DTO gained `LanguageCode` and now serializes `Type` from
  the enum directly instead of the old ad-hoc lowercase-dash strings.
  `AnidbCreator` gained a `Links` list alongside its existing scalar fields.

`fix` (`api`): **Correct group name locking condition in MergeWithExisting**:

The condition had a stray `!` — it read `!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(Name)` when
it should have been `string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(Name)`. This meant the "lock
current name" branch only triggered when a name WAS provided, but then
silently ignored it and used the current group name instead.

With the `!` removed, the behavior is now:

- `HasCustomName: true` without a Name → lock current name as custom
- `HasCustomName: true` with a new Name → falls through to the third
  branch and correctly applies the new name via `update.Name = Name`

This was the root cause of the WebUI rename silently doing nothing.

`fix` (`core`): **Don't reset custom name flag when same name is passed**:

`UpdateGroupInternal` treated passing the same name as the current one
as a "reset to auto-naming" signal (`IsManuallyNamed = 0`). This broke
the "lock current name as custom" scenario and interacted badly with
the `MergeWithExisting` fix.

The fix separates "null name" (reset to auto) from "same name as
current" (keep/lock as custom). Now:
- `update.Name = null` → resets `IsManuallyNamed = 0`, enqueues auto-name
- `update.Name = "SameName"` → keeps `IsManuallyNamed = 1`, no change
- `update.Name = "NewName"` → sets `IsManuallyNamed = 1`, updates name

The same fix is applied to the description handling below it.

`fix` (`api`): **Start PATCH group body from empty defaults**:

The PATCH endpoint seeded the body from the existing group via
`CreateOrUpdateGroupBody(animeGroup)`, copying PreferredSeriesID,
ParentGroupID, SeriesIDs, and GroupIDs into the body. When a user
patched only the Name, all the other seeded fields were also present
and triggered unwanted side effects in MergeWithExisting — like
resetting the preferred series or parent group.

Now the body starts empty; only explicitly patched fields are
non-null and reach MergeWithExisting.

`fix` (`tmdb`): **Reject zero-confidence TMDB matches for anime auto-linking and manual search** ([#1379](https://github.com/ShokoAnime/ShokoServer/pull/1379)):

`AutoSearchForShowUsingTitle`/`AutoSearchMovieUsingTitle` always auto-linked the
top-scored candidate even when it had no title or date correlation to the AniDB
entry at all (`MatchRating.FirstAvailable`) — it only reached scoring because
TMDB's search returned it and it happened to carry the `animation` genre tag.
This let unrelated content (e.g. adult titles genre-tagged as animation on TMDB)
get auto-linked to legitimate anime like "One Piece" purely by coincidence.

- Added `IsAcceptableAutoMatch` to reject `FirstAvailable` before linking in
  both the show and movie auto-match paths, and applied the same floor to the
  transient-outage fallback (which previously guessed blindly when
  `GetTvShowAsync`/`GetMovieAsync` failed for every candidate). This mirrors
  the existing convention in `TmdbLinkingService`'s episode-level matching,
  which already treats `FirstAvailable` as "no match."
- Manual TMDB search (used by the WebUI's link-selection panel) returned TMDB's
  raw search order, which ranks purely on title-text relevance with no genre
  awareness. Added a stable sort (by `animation` genre membership) to the paged
  results in `SearchMoviesRaw`/`SearchShowsRaw` — nothing is filtered out, a
  human still picks the result, this only reorders the page so genuine anime
  surfaces first.
- Extracted the repeated `"animation"` literal into an `AnimationGenre` constant.

This does not affect adult-content handling — `include_adult` is still driven
entirely by `anime.IsRestricted` from AniDB, so correctly-matched restricted
anime are unaffected in both paths. The fix only requires that AniDB's own
title or air-date data actually corroborate the TMDB candidate before
auto-linking, and reorders (without hiding) manual search results by genre.

`feat`: **Add `ThreadPoolMinThreads` setting**:

Raise the .NET thread pool's minimum thread count at startup so bursts
of concurrent work don't stall behind the runtime's gradual
hill-climbing thread injection delay. Configurable via the
THREADPOOL_MIN_THREADS env var; 0 leaves the runtime default
untouched, positive values are used directly, negative values act as
a CPU-count multiplier offset by one (-1 => CPU count x 2, up to -9 =>
CPU count x 10).
`chore` (`abstractions`): **Bump abstractions version to v6.0.0-alpha.68**.

`feat` (`tmdb`): **Add 5XX circuit breaker with escalating backoff and force-run UX** ([#1370](https://github.com/ShokoAnime/ShokoServer/pull/1370)):

Adds a 5XX circuit breaker to `TmdbRateLimiter` that detects sustained TMDB server distress and pauses all TMDB API job dispatch until the server recovers, preventing pointless retry floods. Jobs queued during a pause are held and dispatched automatically when the pause lifts — no work is lost.

**Circuit breaker behaviour**
- A ring buffer tracks the last 3 distress-error timestamps. When all 3 fall within a 10-second window the breaker trips.
- Pause duration escalates with each successive trip: 1 → 3 → 5 → 15 → 60 min (capped at level 5).
- On trip, the ring buffer is cleared so errors from requests already in flight when the breaker tripped don't immediately re-trip and escalate the level further.
- A `Task.Delay`-based timer auto-clears the pause when the duration elapses, so the queue resumes even if no TMDB request is made to call `NotifySuccess` (which would otherwise deadlock behind the acquisition filter).
- The pause also clears on the first successful request after the backoff window passes (`NotifySuccess`), which resets the ramp level and ring buffer. A cheap pre-check skips the lock entirely once the breaker is idle, since every successful TMDB call (up to 10 concurrent) would otherwise funnel through it for nothing.
- 429 backoff and 5XX pause share a single `_backoffUntilTicks` deadline. Both interact safely under `_breakerLock`.
- `GetPauseSnapshot()` reads the paused flag and remaining-time deadline together under the lock, since both are cleared as a pair elsewhere and reading them independently could observe a mid-flip state (paused but no remaining time, or vice versa).

**Job gating**
- New `[TmdbApiRateLimited]` acquisition attribute (extends `[NetworkRequired]` at TMDB worker priority).
- `TmdbApiRateLimitedAcquisitionFilter` blocks all `[TmdbApiRateLimited]` jobs from dispatch while the circuit breaker is tripped, and fires `StateChanged` when the pause toggles so the queue wakes immediately. Implements `IDisposable` via the standard dispose pattern.
- `DownloadTmdbShowImagesJob` and `DownloadTmdbMovieImagesJob` gained `[TmdbApiRateLimited]` (previously missing despite making API calls).
- `PurgeTmdbMovieJob` and `PurgeTmdbShowJob` reverted to `[NetworkRequired]` (they only do local DB deletes, no TMDB API calls).

**Force-run UX**
- When a user force-triggers a refresh or image download while the circuit breaker is active, the job is queued at elevated priority and the endpoint returns **503** with a `Retry-After` header instead of 202. The log emits the approximate wait in seconds.
- The quick-refresh early-return (already updating, episodes present) is answered entirely from local state, so it's checked before the pause gate — it never touches TMDB and shouldn't be refused just because TMDB itself is unavailable.
- `QuickRefresh` is only honored on the immediate execution path (`body.Immediate && body.QuickRefresh`); scheduled/background show refreshes always run the full job with `ForceRefresh` as requested, applied consistently across all force paths.
- Job deduplication in `IQueueScheduler.Enqueue` prevents users from stacking duplicate jobs while paused.

**API surface**
- `TmdbRateLimiter.GetPauseSnapshot()` returns `(IsPaused, Remaining)` as an atomic, lock-protected read so callers never observe a TOCTOU split between the two fields.
- `TmdbMetadataService` exposes `Is5xxPaused`, `RemainingPauseTime`, and `GetPauseSnapshot()` as pass-throughs so the controller doesn't need to inject `TmdbRateLimiter` directly.

**Code quality / concurrency**
- `_5xxPauseLevel` is `volatile` so the lock-free fast-path check in `NotifySuccess` is safe.
- Removed a `fireEvent` variable that was always `true` at its read site (early returns inside the lock exit the lambda entirely, so the log/event only fire on the path where the pause was actually cleared).

`fix` (`tmdb`): **Improve episode title matching for non-English content** ([#1372](https://github.com/ShokoAnime/ShokoServer/pull/1372)):

**Non-English title matching**:
- Expands the TMDB episode title candidates from `en-US` only to `en-US` plus the show's `OriginalLanguageCode` (already stored on `TMDB_Show`), so Japanese-only TMDB episodes can match by title instead of falling through to date-only matching. Titles in other languages are excluded to prevent false-positive fuzzy matches.
- Template titles ("Episode N") are excluded from title candidates in every language — TMDB stores these placeholders per language, but AniDB only stores them in English so they're already filtered on the AniDB side.
- Adds a language fallback for the AniDB episode title lookup: when no English title exists, falls back to the anime's primary/official language (Romaji→Japanese, Pinyin→ChineseSimplified, etc.), rather than skipping title matching entirely.

**Episode matching accuracy fixes**:
- **Episode order reconciliation**: when two AniDB-adjacent episodes both matched on weak evidence alone (date-only or last-resort positional), a coincidental TMDB air-date mismatch could swap them. `ReconcileEpisodeOrderInversions` detects and corrects these after matching, iterating to convergence for runs of 3+ reversed episodes, without disturbing true adjacency between trusted (title-corroborated) matches.
- **Generic placeholder titles**: AniDB's auto-generated placeholder titles ("Episode 5", "Episode S1", "Episode C2", ...) are now excluded from title search for every episode type, not just normal episodes. If both sides only have a placeholder title, the episode isn't left unmatched — title search is simply skipped and the existing air-date and positional-fallback tiers (`TryAirDateMatch` / `FirstAvailable`) still apply, same as before this change.
- **Punctuation normalization**: `~` and `|` are normalized the same as the wave dash and `/`, so decorative/joining-character differences between AniDB and TMDB titles don't silently downgrade an otherwise-identical title to a lower match confidence tier.
- **Greedy candidate starvation**: `TryFindAnidbAndTmdbMatch` now also reports a confidence score (title distance plus air-date corroboration), and each matching pass orders its episodes by that score before matching so the strongest match gets first pick of a shared TMDB episode instead of whichever AniDB episode happened to be processed first. `RankByConfidence` computes this ranking once per pass and reuses the cached match in the pass loop when its chosen TMDB candidate hasn't since been claimed by an earlier (stronger) episode, instead of running the full title-search/air-date match twice per episode.

`refactor`: **Use `Shoko.Abstractions` `AnimeType`/`EpisodeType` directly in v3 models**:

Removed the duplicate `Shoko.Server.API.v3.Models.AniDB.AnimeType` and
`EpisodeType` enums along with their `ToV3Dto` mapping extensions in
`APIv3_Extensions`. `AnidbAnime`, `AnidbEpisode`, and their consumers
now expose the `Shoko.Abstractions.Metadata.Enums` types directly,
serialized via Newtonsoft's global string-enum converter instead of a
per-property `JsonConverter` attribute.

Added `AnimeType.TV` to `Shoko.Abstractions` as the primary value, with
`TVSeries` kept as a legacy compat. alias.

`chore`: **Remove unused imports**.
`chore`: **Disable nullable on APIv1 models and controllers**.
`misc`: **Ensure Long Path Compat in Paths that Matter**.
`misc`: **A lot more safety and improvements to FileSystemWatcher. Cleanup**.

`fix`: **ProcessReleaseProvider job key collision and VideoReleaseService ignoring releaseInfo provided data** ([#1378](https://github.com/ShokoAnime/ShokoServer/pull/1378)):

* fix: remove manual `[JobKeyMember]` to allow automatic JobKey generation

The manual JobKeyMemeber on ProviderID is preventing all other acceptable types to be included in the key, leading to job collisions in providers.

* fix: add support for using provided group information
`misc`: **Make adjustments to Auto-Delete Logic**.

`refactor`: **Remove provider lifecycle events and consolidate release processing**:

Removed the four lifecycle events from the `IReleaseInfoProvider` interface
(`PrepareForSave`, `OnReleaseSaved`, `OnReleaseCleared`, `OnSearchCompleted`)
and consolidated release processing into `IVideoReleaseService` again. Providers
are now purely responsible for matching  again, everything else lives in the
core service layer and applies uniformly to all providers. MyList will be moved
to a separate service using the video release service's events in the future,
but for now, it's the video release's responsibility. Release auto-management
now fires on all save paths, instead of only when ran on auto in the queue.
Added proper chain aborts to the release pipeline. And try schedule rescan for
video builds a single chain for the release providers which were used to match
the last release, ensuring a provider which didn't contribute to the release
info cannot override it afterwards.

`fix`: **Reverse order of entity type sort step for images for entities**.

`chore` (`abstractions`): **Bump abstractions version to v6.0.0-alpha.67**.

`fix`: **Adjust count to index**.

`fix`: **Use pattern matching for `false` check on `bool?`**.

`fix`: **No need to check last attempt winning provider name**.

`misc`: **Counts starts at 1 when non-empty**.

`fix`: **Order match attempts at repo level**.

`chore` (`queue`): **Bump queue processor version to v6.0.0-alpha.5**.
`misc`: **Include Files to Delete in Release Candidates**.

`fix` (`tmdb`): **Try movie search first for short OVA/Web entries** ([#1376](https://github.com/ShokoAnime/ShokoServer/pull/1376)):

Short OVA and Web anime (≤4 main episodes) were routed exclusively to
show search, causing mis-links to the parent franchise's TMDB show when
the correct TMDB representation is standalone movies (e.g. a two-part
film that AniDB models as an OVA series).

Added `IsShortFormAnime` heuristic: for `AnimeType.OVA` and
`AnimeType.Web` with ≤4 `EpisodeType.Episode` entries, movie search
runs first. If it finds matches, each episode is linked independently to
its corresponding TMDB movie via the existing per-episode
`AutoSearchForMovies` path. If movie search returns nothing, the call
falls through to the normal show search so well-formed OVA seasons still
link correctly.

`refactor`: **Centralize image queries in IWithImages, remove per-type image interfaces**:

Deduplicated the boilerplate `IWithImages` implementation that was
copy-pasted across 25+ model classes, each resolving `IImageManager` via
the service locator anti-pattern (`ISystemService.StaticServices`).
Consolidated into default interface methods on `IWithImages` and removed
the per-type image interfaces' inline fallback logic.

Changes to `IWithImages` interface (`IWithImages.cs`):
- Added `Default*ImageCrossReference` nullable properties (Primary,
  Backdrop, Logo, Banner, Disc) with default `null` implementations —
  replacing the `IImage? Default*Image` properties that used to live on
  the per-type interfaces
- Added `GetDefaultImageForType()` / `GetDefaultImages()` — wraps
  default cross-references in `ImageStub.Wrap` with `IsLinkedCrossReference`
- Added `GetDefaultImageCrossReferenceForType()` — switch over
  `ImageEntityType` to dispatch to the correct default xref property
- Added `GetPreferredImages()` / `GetPreferredImageCrossReferences()` —
  enumerates all preferred image type entries
- Added `GetBestImageForType(…)` / `GetBestImages()` — best-image
  fallback: preferred → default → first enabled/available/desired entry
- Added `GetBestImageCrossReferenceForType(…)` / `GetBestImageCrossReferences()` —
  same fallback at the cross-reference level, with a `primaryImage` param
  to branch between primary vs non-primary availability checks

Changes to per-type image interfaces (`IWithPrimaryImage`,
`IWithBackdropImage`, `IWithBannerImage`, `IWithDiscImage`,
`IWithLogoImage`):
- `PrimaryImage` / `BackdropImage` / etc. now delegate to
  `GetBestImageForType(imageType)` instead of repeating inline fallback logic
- `PrimaryImageCrossReference` / `BackdropImageCrossReference` / etc.
  delegate to `GetBestImageCrossReferenceForType(imageType)`
- Removed `DefaultPrimaryImage` / `DefaultBackdropImage` / `DefaultLogoImage` /
  `DefaultBannerImage` / `DefaultDiscImage` (`IImage?` properties) and
  their cross-reference defaults — moved to `IWithImages`

New method on `IImageManager`:
- `IsLinkedCrossReference(entity, xref)` — determines whether a
  cross-reference belongs to the entity itself or is inherited

New implementation in `ImageManager`:
- `IsLinkedCrossReference` uses `TryGetMetadataForEntity` to compare
  source/type/ID triple

Model classes stripped of duplicate `IWithImages` implementations (25
classes):
- Removed `GetPreferredImageForType`, `GetPreferredImageCrossReferenceForType`,
  `GetImages`, `GetImageCrossReferences` from: `AniDB_Anime`,
  `AniDB_Character`, `AniDB_Creator`, `AniDB_Episode`, `AnimeEpisode`,
  `AnimeSeries`, `JMMUser`, `VideoLocal`, `CrossRef_AniDB_TMDB_Episode`,
  `CrossRef_AniDB_TMDB_Movie`, `CrossRef_AniDB_TMDB_Season`,
  `CrossRef_AniDB_TMDB_Show`, `TMDB_AlternateOrdering_Episode`,
  `TMDB_AlternateOrdering_Season`, `TMDB_Collection`, `TMDB_Company`,
  `TMDB_Episode`, `TMDB_Movie`, `TMDB_Person`, `TMDB_Season`,
  `TMDB_Show`, `TMDB_Studio`, `AniDB_Season`, `AniDB_Studio`
- Removed `DefaultPrimaryImage` (`IImage?` wrapper) from all of the above
- Removed `DefaultBackdropImage`, `DefaultLogoImage`, `DefaultBannerImage`,
  `DefaultDiscImage` from entities that had them
- Removed `BackdropImage`, `BackdropImageCrossReference`, `BannerImage`,
  `BannerImageCrossReference`, `DiscImage`, `DiscImageCrossReference`,
  `LogoImage`, `LogoImageCrossReference` from `TMDB_AlternateOrdering_Episode`
  and `AnimeGroup`
- Removed `GetPreferredImages()` from `AnimeSeries` and `AnimeGroup`
  (replaced by `IWithImages.GetBestImages()`)

`AnimeGroup` override (`GetBestImageCrossReferenceForType`):
- Custom override that checks the group's own preferred images first,
  then falls through to the main series' preferred/default images, then
  to the group's own image collection — preserving the original
  `GetGroupImageCrossReference` semantics

`AnimeSeries` changes:
- Removed `GetPreferredImages()` method
- `AvailableImageTypes` / `PreferredImageTypes` now cast to `(IWithImages)`
  to call interface defaults
- Removed `DefaultPrimaryImage` (was delegating to `AniDB_Anime`)

API callers updated to cast to `(IWithImages)` before calling interface
methods:
- `EpisodeController`, `GroupController`, `SeriesController`,
  `TmdbController` (all v3 image endpoints)
- v3 API models: `Episode`, `Group`, `Series`, `TmdbEpisode`,
  `TmdbMovie`, `TmdbSeason`, `TmdbShow`, `Studio`
- v1 provider implementation (`TMDB_Movie` image queries)
- v2 models: `Episode`, `Filter`, `Group`, `Serie`
- `GeneratedPlaylistService`: replaced `GetPreferredImageForType ??
  DefaultPrimaryImage` with `GetBestImageForType`
- v2 `Filter`: replaced `anime.AniDB_Anime?.DefaultPrimaryImage` with
  `series.GetDefaultImageForType(ImageEntityType.Primary)`
- v3 `Group` model: uses `GetBestImages()` instead of `GetPreferredImages()`
- v3 `Series` model: uses `GetBestImages()` instead of `GetPreferredImages()`

`chore`: **Remove nullable usage in nullable disabled files**.

`chore`: **Sort imports and fix line endings**.

`refactor`: **Consolidate image/xref filter params into options objects**:

Replaced the spread individual-parameter signatures on IImageManager and
IWithImages with three dedicated options classes:
ImageFilteringOptions, ImageCrossReferenceFilteringOptions, and
RandomImageCrossReferenceFilteringOptions.

Added new filter fields alongside the migration:
- IsPrimaryImage (bool?) — filter by PrimaryID == ID
- AsPrimaryImage (bool) — redirect to the canonical primary via
  GetImageByID(xref.ImageID, primaryImage: true)
- IsPrimaryAvailable (bool?) — filter on cross-reference primary
  availability
- IsPreferred on ImageFilteringOptions — for xref subquery filtering
  in image-level queries

Migrated all .FirstOrDefault(x => x.IsPreferred) post-filter callers to
use IsPreferred = true in the options object with a naked
.FirstOrDefault().

Stripped redundant per-parameter XML doc in the interface files, replacing
it with shorter blocks that link to the options class.

Future filter additions now require one property on the options class plus
one line in the implementation — no cascading through 25 model overrides
or 30+ caller files.

`chore`: **Enable nullable across all projects and all files**.

`chore` (`abstractions`): **Bump abstractions version to v6.0.0-alpha.66**.

`chore`: **Fix nullable warnings**.

`chore`: **Fix nullable warnings in Services**.
`misc`: **Sort the Deletion Preview**.
`fix`: **Fix a case of multiple releases in the same group not showing**.
`fix`: **Correct distinct by for primary images**.
`refactor`: **Tweak group preferred image lookup internals**.
